question_html:
Make meaningful sentences with the following words:

Traumatize, Acquaintance, Trust-worthy, Charity, Hyperbole
solution_html:
Traumatize: The accident traumatized the children and they needed counseling.

Acquaintance: I met an old acquaintance at the reunion last weekend.

Trust-worthy: She is a trust-worthy friend who always keeps her promises.

Charity: She helps others out of¬Ýcharity.

Hyperbole: His story was full of hyperbole.

Marks Distribution:

(0.5√ó5) = 2.5 marks for making meaningful and correct sentences with the given words.

question_html:
Fill in the gaps with appropriate words:

The coach insisted that the player (a) ‚Äî‚Äî (arrive) early for practice. Neither the player nor his teammates (b) ‚Äî‚Äî (be) responsible for the delay, as their car had broken down. They had the mechanic (c) ‚Äî‚Äî (repair) the engine. If he (d) ‚Äî‚Äî (speak) politely, the coach wouldn‚Äôt have been angry. He needs to fix his attitude lest he (e) ‚Äî‚Äî (mess) up his career.
solution_html:
(a) arrive (b) were (c) repair (d) had spoken (e) should mess

Marks Distribution:

(0.5√ó5) = 2.5 marks for filling in the gaps with appropriate words.

question_html: Write a paragraph on ‚ÄòRisk of Substance Abuse in Adolescence‚Äô.
solution_html:
Risk of Substance Abuse in Adolescence

Adolescence is a critical stage of life when young people go through physical, emotional, and mental changes. At this time, many teenagers become curious and are easily influenced by peers and social media. One serious risk they often face is substance abuse. Many adolescents start experimenting with smoking, drugs, or alcohol without understanding the harmful effects. This habit can damage their health, disturb their studies, and ruin their future. Family problems, lack of awareness, and bad company often lead to such behavior. Once addicted, it becomes difficult to quit, and it may cause mental illness, crime, and even death. To prevent this, schools and families should guide the young and make them aware of the dangers. It is important for adolescents to make wise choices and focus on building a better future. Proper care and support can help them stay safe and healthy.

Marks Distribution

0.5 mark for writing the title: ‚ÄòRisk of Substance Abuse in Adolescence‚Äô.

01 mark for writing the introduction to adolescence and its challenges.

1.5 marks for the explanation of causes of substance abuse in teens.

01 mark for the explanation of effects on health, education, and future.

01 mark for the explanation of preventive measures and conclusion.

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the deserved marks.

*0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two grammar or spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more grammar or spelling mistakes.]
